response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but the profile is a fundamental mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect role centered on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator design, and hardware-software co-design. There is no evidence of silicon architecture work, GPU/accelerator workload analysis, or deep-learning performance optimization. The closest relevant experience — low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ work at National Instruments — falls well short of the hardware-level depth required at principal level.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, caching)
Broad technical range across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems with strong testing discipline
Meets the raw experience bar (BS + 15+ years) and shows sustained open-source and teaching contributions
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance-modeling experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/application software; no signal of SIMD/MIMD, computer architecture, or power/performance trade-off work
